file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Shipment Tracking page of the application. Contains all UI element locators for tracking ink cartridge shipments, paper deliveries, FAQ sections, shipment history tables, and support resources. Provides methods to interact with FAQ questions, retrieve page title, and access various tracking-related elements. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Shipment Tracking page where users can view the status of their ink cartridge and paper shipments. Contains locators for different cartridge types (K, C, M, Y, CMY), paper tracking items, FAQ sections with expandable questions and answers, shipment history table with date/description/tracking number columns, and support links for recycling cartridges and reporting missing ink. Provides methods to wait for page load, access specific FAQ questions by index, and retrieve element selectors dynamically. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns.

locators.ui_element_description: FAQ accordion button elements that expand/collapse to show answers. Multiple buttons exist on the page for different FAQ questions.
First FAQ answer content container that appears when the first FAQ question is expanded.
Second FAQ answer content container that appears when the second FAQ question is expanded.
Third FAQ answer content container that appears when the third FAQ question is expanded.
Fourth FAQ answer content container that appears when the fourth FAQ question is expanded.
Tracking card displaying shipment information for the Black (K) ink cartridge.
Tracking card displaying shipment information for the Cyan (C) ink cartridge.
Tracking card displaying shipment information for the Magenta (M) ink cartridge.
Tracking card displaying shipment information for the Yellow (Y) ink cartridge.
Tracking card displaying shipment information for the combined CMY (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow) ink cartridge.
Container card displaying shipment tracking information for paper deliveries.
Main page title heading for the Ink Shipment tracking page, located within the ink-shipment-ucde section.
FAQ card container that holds all frequently asked questions and answers related to ink shipment.
Link within the FAQ section that navigates to the plan overview page for more details about the subscription plan.
Link within the FAQ section that navigates to the change plan page where users can modify their subscription plan.
Link within the fourth FAQ answer (item-3) that provides information about recycling ink cartridges.
Support card container that provides additional help and resources related to ink cartridges.
Link that navigates to the cartridge recycling program page where users can learn how to recycle used cartridges.
Link that allows users to report missing ink cartridges or request replacement for lost shipments.
Date column header button in the shipment history table that can be clicked to sort entries by date.
Description column header in the shipment history table showing shipment activity descriptions.
Tracking number column in the shipment history table, typically displayed as a clickable link to carrier tracking page.
Dropdown selector control in the shipment history table that allows filtering or selecting different shipment records.
Dropdown options list that appears when the table selector is clicked, showing available filter options.
Main container for the shipment history table displaying all past and current shipment records with dates, descriptions, and tracking numbers.
Activity description text elements within the shipment history table rows that describe each shipment event or status update.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the ShipmentTrackingPage by storing the Playwright page instance in self.page and creating an ElementsDict instance to hold all page element locators. Then systematically defines 26 different UI element locators covering FAQ sections (questions and 4 answer containers), cartridge tracking cards for all ink types (K, C, M, Y, CMY), paper tracking items, page title, FAQ card container, FAQ navigation links (overview, update plan, recycle), support resources (recycle link, missing ink link), and shipment history table components (date column, description column, tracking number column, table selector dropdown, selector options list, table container, and activity descriptions). All locators are stored as attributes of the self.elements dictionary-like object for organized access throughout the class methods.

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds to wait for the page title element to appear. Defaults to 60000ms (60 seconds).

functions.what_it_does: Waits for the page title element to be present in the DOM and returns the element handle once found. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method with the page_title locator selector string ('#ink-shipment-ucde [data-testid="page-title"]') and the provided timeout value. This method is typically used to verify that the Shipment Tracking page has fully loaded before proceeding with other interactions. Returns the Playwright ElementHandle for the page title element which can then be used for further operations like getting text content or verifying visibility.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the FAQ question to retrieve. For example, 0 for the first question, 1 for the second question, etc.

functions.docstring: Returns the locator for a specific FAQ question by index (0-based).
functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for a specific FAQ question button element based on the provided zero-based index. First creates a locator for all FAQ question buttons using self.page.locator(self.elements.faq_question) which matches all buttons within the accordion with selector '[data-testid="accordion"] button'. Then uses the .nth(index) method to select the specific question at the given index position. This allows tests to interact with individual FAQ questions by their position in the list, such as clicking to expand a specific question or verifying its text content. The returned locator can be used for click actions, text retrieval, or visibility checks.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: String name of the selector to retrieve from the elements dictionary, such as 'page_title', 'login_button', 'faq_card', etc.

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a selector string from the elements dictionary by name. First checks if the provided name exists as a key in the self.elements dictionary using the 'in' operator. If the name exists, returns the corresponding selector string value from self.elements[name]. If the name does not exist in the elements dictionary, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message indicating that the selector name was not found in this page object class. This method provides a safe way to access selectors dynamically with proper error handling, which is useful when selector names are determined at runtime or passed as parameters from test data.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object follows a clean pattern using ElementsDict to organize all locators. It contains 26 locators covering comprehensive shipment tracking functionality including multiple cartridge types (K, C, M, Y, CMY), FAQ sections with 4 answer containers, shipment history table with multiple columns, and support links. The faq_question method provides dynamic access to FAQ questions by index using Playwright's nth() selector. The get_selector method provides runtime selector retrieval with error handling. This page extends BasePageObject but does not call super().__init__() in the constructor, instead directly assigning self.page and self.elements. The page is focused on read-only tracking information display rather than form interactions.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Shipping and Billing management page of the application. Contains all UI element locators for managing shipping addresses and payment methods including form modals, input fields, validation messages, and action buttons. Provides methods to interact with shipping address forms, billing payment forms, and account type selection. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Shipping and Billing management page where users can add, edit, and manage their shipping addresses and payment methods. Contains locators organized into three sections: shipping section (manage address link, form modal, address fields, validation messages, save/cancel buttons), billing section (manage payment link, billing form modal, payment method fields, PayPal logo, credit card inputs, iframe elements), and page-level elements (page title, notification messages, modal close buttons). Provides a static method to select business account type during billing setup. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and the elements dictionary structure.

locators.ui_element_description: Link or button to open the shipping address management modal where users can add or edit their shipping address.
Modal dialog container that displays the shipping address form with all input fields for entering or editing shipping information.
Main container section on the page that displays the current shipping address and management options.
Go Back button that appears in the unsupported postal code error modal when a user enters a shipping address in an unsupported region.
Error message text element that displays validation errors or issues with shipping address or billing information entry.
First name text input field in the shipping address form where users enter the recipient's first name.
ZIP code or postal code text input field in the shipping address form.
Street address line 1 text input field in the shipping address form for entering the primary street address.
State or province input field within the shipping address form, typically a dropdown or autocomplete input.
Radio button or checkbox option to select the suggested/corrected address when address validation suggests an alternative.
Radio button or checkbox option to keep the original address as entered when address validation suggests an alternative.
Save button in the shipping address form modal that submits the entered address information.
Confirmation button to finalize shipping to the selected or entered address.
Paragraph element displaying the currently saved shipping address information on the main page.
Cancel button in the shipping address form modal that closes the modal without saving changes.

Link or button to open the payment method management modal where users can add or edit their billing payment information. Uses a composite selector matching either the manage payment text or the add billing button.
Main container section on the page that displays the current payment method and billing management options.
Modal dialog container that displays the billing payment method form with credit card and billing address input fields.
Button to add a new payment method when no payment method is currently saved.
Address section within the billing form modal where users enter their billing address information.
PayPal logo image element that appears as a payment option in the billing section.
Main page title heading for the Shipping and Billing management page.
Generic cancel button that appears in various modals to close without saving changes.
Notification message container that displays success or error messages after updating shipping or billing information.
Close (X) button in the top corner of modal dialogs to dismiss the modal.
Street address line 1 input field in the billing address section of the billing form modal.
Street address line 2 input field (optional) in the billing address section for apartment, suite, or unit numbers.
Continue button in multi-step forms to proceed to the next step after entering required information.
Credit card number input field in the billing form where users enter their 16-digit card number.
Inline error message that appears below billing address fields when validation fails.
Error message element that displays when an invalid credit card number or payment information is entered.

Iframe element that loads the secure payment gateway or 3D Secure challenge frame for credit card processing. Uses composite selector to match either the payment gateway iframe or the challenge frame.
Next or Submit button within the payment gateway iframe to finalize adding the credit card.
Radio button to select Business as the account type when setting up billing information.
Company name text input field that appears when Business account type is selected.
Icon image that displays the detected credit card type (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, etc.) based on the entered card number.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the ShippingBillingPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) which sets up the page instance and creates the elements dictionary. Then systematically defines 38 UI element locators organized into three logical sections. The shipping section includes 15 locators for managing shipping addresses: manage link, form modal, section container, error handling, input fields (first name, zip code, street, state), address validation options (suggested/original), action buttons (save, cancel, ship to address), and saved address display. The billing section includes 17 locators for managing payment methods: manage payment link, billing section container, form modal, add payment button, address section, PayPal logo, billing address inputs (street1, street2), card number input, error messages (inline and card validation), payment gateway iframe, next button, business account selection (radio button, company name field), and card type icon. The page elements section includes 6 general locators: page title, cancel button, notification messages, modal close button, and continue button. All locators are stored as attributes of the inherited self.elements dictionary for organized access.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to perform the action on

functions.docstring: Selects the business account type radio button
functions.what_it_does: Selects the business account type by clicking the business radio button in the billing form. This is a static method that takes a Playwright page instance as a parameter rather than using self. It creates a locator for the business radio button using the selector '[data-testid="business-radio-button"]', then uses .first to get the first matching element (in case multiple exist), and performs a click action with force=True to ensure the click succeeds even if the element is not in the viewport or is obscured. The force=True parameter bypasses Playwright's actionability checks. This method is typically called during billing setup when the user needs to specify they are setting up a business account rather than a personal account, which then reveals additional fields like company name.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object is well-organized with clear separation of shipping and billing sections through inline comments. It contains 38 locators making it one of the more comprehensive page objects, covering complete shipping address management and payment method management workflows. The page properly calls super().__init__() to inherit BasePageObject functionality. It includes one static method select_business_account_type which is unusual - typically page object methods are instance methods, but this static approach allows calling without instantiating the page object. The locators use a mix of data-testid (most reliable), class selectors with ^ prefix matching (for styled components), and ID selectors. Several locators use composite selectors with commas to match multiple possible elements. The page handles complex scenarios like address validation (suggested vs original), payment gateway iframes, and business vs personal account types. Notable that some locators have duplicate or similar purposes (cancel_button vs cancel_address_button, error_message vs billing_address_error_message) suggesting different contexts or form sections.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Sign In page of the application. Contains UI element locators for the sign-in form including email input, password input, and submit buttons. Provides the basic structure for sign-in page interactions. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements for the Sign In page where users authenticate to access the application. Contains locators for the email/username input field, the button to proceed to password entry, the password input field, and the final sign-in submit button. This page object represents a two-step sign-in flow where users first enter their email/username, then click to proceed to password entry, then enter their password and submit. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and the elements dictionary structure. Also imports test_flows_common module which likely contains reusable test flow helper functions.

locators.ui_element_description: Email or username text input field where users enter their account identifier in the first step of the sign-in process.
Submit button for the username/email form that proceeds to the password entry step. Clicked after entering the email to move to the next step of authentication.
Password text input field where users enter their password in the second step of the sign-in process. This is a masked input field.
Final sign-in submit button that submits the password and completes the authentication process.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the SignInPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) which sets up the page instance and creates the elements dictionary. Then defines 4 UI element locators for the two-step sign-in flow. First defines self.elements.email_input with selector '#username' for the email/username input field. Then defines self.elements.use_password_button with selector '#user-name-form-submit' for the button that proceeds from email entry to password entry. Then defines self.elements.password_input with selector '#password' for the password input field. Finally defines self.elements.sign_in_button with selector '#sign-in' for the final submit button. All locators use simple ID selectors which are reliable and performant. The locators are stored as attributes of the inherited self.elements dictionary for organized access throughout any methods that would be added to this class.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a minimal page object with only the constructor defined and no action or verification methods. It contains 4 locators representing a two-step sign-in flow (email entry -> proceed button -> password entry -> sign-in button). All locators use ID selectors which are the most reliable selector type. The page imports test_flows_common module but does not use it in the constructor, suggesting that common sign-in flows might be implemented in that module rather than as methods in this page object class. This design pattern separates page element definitions (in page objects) from test flows (in separate modules). The lack of methods like enter_email(), enter_password(), or perform_sign_in() suggests these interactions are handled elsewhere, possibly in the test_flows_common module or directly in test scripts. This is a valid but less encapsulated approach compared to page objects that include both locators and interaction methods.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Thank You confirmation page of the application. Contains UI element locators for the thank you message card, continue button, email display, cartridge images, subtitle text, and modal close button. Represents the final confirmation page shown after completing a workflow such as printer replacement or subscription setup. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements for the Thank You confirmation page that appears after users complete important workflows like printer replacement, subscription enrollment, or order completion. Contains locators for the continue button to proceed to the next step, the thank you card container displaying confirmation information, a modal close button for finishing printer replacement workflows, the email address display showing where confirmation was sent, cartridge images showing what was ordered or configured, and subtitle text with additional confirmation details. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and the elements dictionary structure. This page serves as a confirmation and transition point in multi-step workflows.

locators.ui_element_description: Continue button on the thank you page that allows users to proceed to the next step or return to the main application after viewing the confirmation.
Main thank you card container displaying the confirmation message and details. Located in the right section of the thank-you-step component.
Close button in the modal footer that appears during printer replacement workflow completion. Clicking this button closes the modal and finishes the printer replacement process.
Email address text element displaying the email address where the confirmation was sent.
Image element displaying the ink cartridge(s) that were ordered, configured, or included in the completed workflow.
Subtitle text element providing additional information or instructions on the thank you confirmation card.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the ThankYouPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) which sets up the page instance and creates the elements dictionary. Then defines 6 UI element locators for the thank you confirmation page. First defines self.elements.continue_button with selector '[data-testid="continue-button"]' for the button to proceed after viewing confirmation. Then defines self.elements.thank_you_card with composite selector '[data-testid="thank-you-step"] [class^="styles__RightSection"]' for the main confirmation card container. Then defines self.elements.close_modal_finish_printer_replacement with CSS selector '.vn-modal--footer > div> button' for the modal close button in printer replacement workflows. Then defines self.elements.email with class selector '[class^="styles__Email-"]' for the email address display. Then defines self.elements.cartridge_image with class selector '[class^="styles__Cartridge-"]' for cartridge images. Finally defines self.elements.subtitle with class selector '[class^="styles__InfoCardSubtitle-"]' for subtitle text. All locators are stored as attributes of the inherited self.elements dictionary for organized access.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a simple confirmation page object with only the constructor defined and no action or verification methods. It contains 6 locators representing elements typically found on a thank you/confirmation page. Most locators use class selectors with ^ prefix matching for styled components, except continue_button which uses data-testid (most reliable). The thank_you_card locator uses a composite selector combining data-testid and class matching. The close_modal_finish_printer_replacement locator has a very specific name suggesting it's used in a particular workflow context (printer replacement). Like the SignInPage, this page object only defines locators without interaction methods, suggesting interactions are handled in test scripts or separate flow modules. The page serves as a confirmation point in workflows, so typical interactions would be clicking continue or verifying displayed information (email, cartridge images, subtitle text).

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Terms of Service HP Smart page of the application. Contains the UI element locator for the continue button that users click to accept terms of service and proceed. Represents a consent/agreement page in the user onboarding or setup flow. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates the UI element for the Terms of Service HP Smart consent page where users must accept terms and conditions before proceeding with HP Smart application setup or usage. Contains a single locator for the continue button that users click to accept the terms of service and move forward in the onboarding or setup flow. This is a simple consent page with minimal interaction - users review the terms and click continue to accept. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and the elements dictionary structure. The page is part of the initial setup or first-time user experience flow.

locators.ui_element_description: Continue button in the footer of the full-screen consent form that users click to accept the Terms of Service and proceed with HP Smart setup or usage.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the TermsOfServiceHPSmartPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) which sets up the page instance and creates the elements dictionary. Then defines a single UI element locator for the continue button. Sets self.elements.continue_button with the ID selector '#full-screen-consent-form-footer-button-continue' which targets the continue button in the footer of the full-screen consent form. This button is the primary (and only) interactive element on this page - users click it to accept the terms of service and proceed. The locator uses an ID selector which is reliable and performant. The locator is stored as an attribute of the inherited self.elements dictionary for organized access.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the simplest page object in the batch with only 1 locator and no action methods. It represents a straightforward consent page where the only user action is clicking continue to accept terms. The single locator uses an ID selector which is the most reliable selector type. The ID is very descriptive: 'full-screen-consent-form-footer-button-continue' clearly indicates this is a continue button in the footer of a full-screen consent form. Like other page objects in this batch, it only defines locators without interaction methods, following the pattern of separating element definitions from test flows. The page name 'TermsOfServiceHPSmartPage' indicates this is specifically for HP Smart application terms of service, suggesting the application has multiple terms of service pages for different contexts or products. This page would typically appear early in user onboarding or first-time setup flows.

ShipmentTrackingPage.faq_question — Returns locator for specific FAQ question by zero-based index
ShipmentTrackingPage.get_selector — Retrieves selector string by name with error handling
ShippingBillingPage.select_business_account_type — Static method to select business account type radio button

ShipmentTrackingPage.cCartridgeItem — Cyan ink cartridge tracking card
ShipmentTrackingPage.mCartridgeItem — Magenta ink cartridge tracking card
ShipmentTrackingPage.yCartridgeItem — Yellow ink cartridge tracking card
ShipmentTrackingPage.cmyCartridgeItem — CMY combined cartridge tracking card

ShippingBillingPage.shipping_form_modal — Shipping address form modal container
ShippingBillingPage.manage_your_payment_method_link — Link to open payment method management
ShippingBillingPage.billing_form_modal — Billing payment form modal container
